details.text:
- “It (i.e., the Kitab) contains a mention of you” meaning that it contains that which relates to you and your realities that you can recognise.
- A second possible meaning is: “It contains your way of doing Dhikr (remembrance of Allah)” i.e., what you should do.
- A third meaning not mentioned by Ustadh Nu’man but mentioned by many Mufassireen is: “It contains the means for you to attain honour and to elevate yourselves and standing”.

Derivative: زَفِيرٌ is a very loud exhale sound and it can also refer to a mule that gets louder and louder due to the heavy load it is carrying. زَفِيرٌ is only used for living creatures in Arabic however the Qur’an describes the fire of hell with the qualities of a living creature.
Derivative: مُبْعَدُونَ is in the اِسْمُ المَفْعُول (the object of the action) from أُفْعَلَ – يُفْعَلُ and means: They will be distanced from it.
Derivative: حَسِيس the quiet sound of footsteps or a burning flame
- What ever whim came to their mind (or whatever they wish, desire of crave for).
- The great most terrible fear or terror.
Derivative: طَوَى – يَطْوِي – طَيّ means to wrap or fold up
Derivative: أُذُن means an ear and آذَنَ means to fill someone’s ear, to make sure to be heard (i.e., to make known or proclaim)
Derivative: قَصَمَ means to shatter, smash to pieces and destroy.
Derivative: المُسْتَعَانُ is the only One whose help is sought and relied upon.
Derivative: رَكَضَ means to hit your heels on the horse to make it hurry and in modern day it is used to mean to run.
Derivative: أَتْرَفَ - يُتْرِفُ – إِتْراف means to have a lot of wealth, to enjoy it and live an elite lifestyle.
Derivative: حَصِيد refers to a farm field after the harvesting has been done and nothing but little bits of stalks remain.
Derivative: خَمَدَ means for the life within something to wither away and die.
Derivative: لاعِبِينَ – It mean to play around i.e., as a game.
Derivative: زاهِق from زَهَقَ means to run in defeat, to be no more and to die away.
Derivative: دَمَغَ literally to bash the brains out and it used to mean that it irrefutably refutes.
Derivative: قَذَفَ to hurl

نَقْذِفُ بِٱلْحَقِّ عَلَى ٱلْبَٰطِلِ فَيَدْمَغُهُۥ فَإِذَا هُوَ زَاهِقٌ

- The use of قَذَفَ (to hurl) indicates to the distance between truth and falsehood.
- The fact that the truth (الحَقّ) smashed the brains (يَدْمَغُ) of the falsehood (بَاطِل) means that the truth has absolutely no tolerance for the falsehood.
it indicates that the truth should always be on the offensive.
Derivative: حَسَرٌ means exhaustion and tirednessاِسْتَحْسَرَ to become tired and grow weary.

Derivative: يَفْتُرُونَ from فَتَرَ – يَفْتُرُ – فُتُوراً: It means to start off with a lot of energy or strong and then begin to languish and slacken. لَا يَفْتُرُونَ (they do not languish or slacken) means that they are persistent, continuous and constant.

- يَصفُون is from وَصَفَ - وَصْف and it means to describe something in elaborate detail. عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ means: Concerning their elaborate characterisations and descriptions i.e., in relation to the Gods that they invent.
Derivative: هاتِ and هَاتُوا is a verb that only has the imperative form and means to bring something with immediacy and urgency.

- Here يَقُلْ is in the مَجْزوم (Majzum) form and whenever this form is used without a factor or cause (عامل) to make it so, then it means that a hypothetical question or situation is being presented. Instead of مَنْ يَقولُ “Whoever says”, it states مَن يَقُلْ and means “If anyone would say” i.e., a hypothetical supposition.
Derivative: لَاهِيَة comes from لَهَى which means to be distracted, heedless and just wanting to play and be amused.
Derivative: رَتْقًا is from رَتَقَ and means to be fused (and joined) together.
Derivative: فَتَقَ is the opposite of رَتَقَ and means to be torn apart or rendered and split open.

Derivative: رَواسي is the plural of الراسي and is derived from رَسا – يَرْسو which means to anchor. Mountains are compared to ships which lay their anchors down. رَواسي is also used to refer to mountain ranges.
Derivative: فِجَاجًا refers to vast valleys. When the pathway between mountains is vast and open it is called فِجَاجًا.
Derivative: سُبُلًا is the plural of سَبِيلٌ and means the most convenient road or path for travelling through.
Derivative: تَمِيدَ comes from the verb مَادَ which means for something to totter and then tip over.
Derivative: سَقْفًا means a ceiling from this inside as opposed to the roof which is on the outside.
Derivative: مَحْفُوظ means to be guarded and has a lot of protection in it.
Derivative: فَلَك is an orbit; something that has a round path and fulfils a circuit.

Derivative: فِتْنَةً (as a test) has origins in the process of purifying gold which requires high temperatures and as such holds the meaning of being tested intensely and being put under the flame quite a bit.
- مِنْ عَجَلٍ means with the ingredients of rushing or being hasty and the inclination to want things quickly or to happen quickly.
Derivative: كَفَّ – يَكُفُّ means to raise your palm to defend yourself which means to hold back and prevent.
Derivative: بَغْتَةً means something that comes, descends or happens unexpectedly, suddenly, unimagined and in surprise.
Derivative: بَهِتَ – يَبْهَتُ – بَهْت: To be shocked into silence and flabbergasted.
Derivative: اِسْتِهْزاء means to make fun of, mock or ridicule privately, behind their back or in closed circles and not in someone’s face or openly.
Derivative: حَاقَ means to descend, drop upon and surround.
Derivative: سَخِرَ means to ridicule and make fun in order to humiliate someone publicly.
Derivative: كَلَأَ – يَكْلَأُ means to keep constant watch over something or someone for the purpose of protecting and guarding.
Derivative: يُصْحَبُونَ means that they will not be given company, which means as an escort for the purpose of protection.

- It literally means that we approach the land decreasing it from its sides. It means: (do not they see) that we are shrinking or will shrink their world (which they have enjoyed) and their sphere of influence due to the victories of Islam and its spread.
Derivative: نَفْحَة is cold draft or cool breeze.
Derivative: مَسَّ means to touch but lightly or to barely touch.
Derivative: وَيْل is the worst possible thing that can happen to someone.
- O what terrible destruction has befallen us!

Derivative: ٱلْقِسْطَ is a مَصْدَر (Masdar - An infinitive) but it is being used as an adjective here in the phrase ٱلْمَوَٰزِينَ ٱلْقِسْطَ (The truly just scales). When the scales are described with an infinitive it implies that you will never find more justice than these scales. The scales are not only just but they reflect justice themselves.

Derivative: ٱلْفُرْقَان comes from فَرْق which means to separate. The أَلِف and نُون (ان) at the end of ٱلْفُرْقَان makes its meaning exaggerated, so that it carries the meaning of the extremely powerful separator, that which clearly separates the right from the wrong.
Derivative: ضِياء refers to a warm light, like the sunlight whilst نُور (light) refers to a cool light. The light that can have an element of toughness or discomfort within it is ضِياء.

Derivative: مُشْفِق is from إِشْفاق which means to be overwhelmed by fear. It is also considered to fall under the category of the language of الأضداد (opposites) where a form of the word can have the opposite meaning. شَفيق refers to someone who is extremely merciful (compassionate and sympathetic) who removes your fears whilst مُشْفِق is someone who can instil fear in you or it means that you are terribly afraid.
Derivative: أَضْغاث is when something is muddled, confused and mixed up, like about a story.
- أَضْغاثُ أَحْلَامٍ means mixed up and jumbled dreams and can be a reference to crazy nightmares.
Derivative: مُّبَارَكٌ is from بَرَكَة which is the increase in good above expectation.
Derivative: تَمْثِيل is a representation i.e., the تَمَاثِيل are the representation of something greater that they worship.
- تَاللهِ (I swear by Allah) is stronger than والله and is used in a serious situation.
Derivative: جُذاذ comes from جِذٌّ and it means the smallest fragment of something. جُذاذ means into small pieces and fragments.
Derivative: يَنطِقُونَ from نَطَقَ which means to utter a word.
- “Thery looked deeply within (or examined) themselves”.
Derivative: نُكِسُوا from نَكَسَ means to lower.

- “Their heads were made to lower (or hang down) in shame”.
another meaning held by the majority of the Mufassireen is that it means: “That they relapsed upon themselves i.e., to their ways of disbelief after the Previous realisation of their wrongdoing”.
Derivative: أُفٍّ is an expression of disgust in response to something rotten and shameful or ugly.
Derivative: حَرَّقَ – تَحْرِيق means to burn something to bits.
Derivative: نَافِلَةً meaning as an additional granting or bonus – something extra on top.
Derivative: سَوْءٍ refers to an ugly evil.
- قَوْمَ سَوْءٍ refers to a people or nation that has been marked by evil.
Derivative: نَفَشَ is a specific word for the reality of sheep escaping their pen and going to graze outside of it specifically during the night.
-the manufacture of body armour.
Derivative: عاصِفَة means to rage (storm) violently and to be destructive to whatever is in its path.
Derivative: يَغُوصُ means to dive deep into the sea.

Derivative: مُغَٰضِبًا can imply more than just anger (غَضَبٌ. It can imply a state of mutual anger. مُغاضِب is the اسم الفاعل (noun of the doer) from غاضب – مُغاضَبَة from the فَاعَلَ form which indicates that the anger is mutual.
Derivative: حَدَب is anything that is a rise or elevation of the earth.
Derivative: نَسَلَ means to run down a slope.
Derivative: شَاخِصَة from شَخَصَ which means not being able to blink and for the eyes to be fixed (i.e., staring).
Derivative: Small pebbles or stones that burn.
